items.title: US Jobless Claims Decline to 208,000, Below Forecasts
items.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week, suggesting the labor market remains stable.

items.title: Multistrat Hedge Fund Paloma to Cut Teams in Latest Pivot
items.description: Donald Sussman, the veteran behind Paloma Partners, is pivoting his long-standing multistrategy hedge fund to focus on fewer managers in which he has the highest conviction as the firm adjusts to a de

items.title: SMBC Bolsters Global Sales and Trading Push With Macro Hire
items.description: Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. hired Kunal Maini as international head of macro rates, bolstering efforts to expand its sales and trading franchise beyond its core Japanese market.

items.title: US Drivers Are Again Paying More Than $5 a Gallon for Diesel
items.description: American drivers are once again paying more than $5 a gallon for diesel, with a fresh round of hostilities in the Middle East raising the specter of more war-induced inflation.

items.title: US Stock Futures Today: AtaiBeckley, GE Aerospace, UnitedHealth
items.description: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.2% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York. The US intensified strikes against Iran, hitting an oil tanker near the country’s main export terminal for the first time since the res

items.title: Abbott Boosts 2026 Profit Guidance as Sales Across Firm Rise
items.description: Abbott Laboratories raised its 2026 profit guidance after a stronger-than-expected second quarter driven by improved performance across most of its business lines.

items.title: US Power Grid Faces Defining Test for AI Boom
items.description: The operator of America’s biggest electricity network is entering an emergency process to plug a projected supply shortfall.

items.title: Retail Traders’ Rise Upends the Established Stock Market Order
items.description: Retail traders have moved from the stock market’s margins to its center.

items.title: Utility Warns Blast at South Africa Water Plant to Hit Supply
items.description: An explosion on power lines that feed a key water-pumping hub risks cutting supply to parts of South Africa’s commercial hub of Johannesburg and key platinum- and gold-mining towns to the city’s west,

items.title: Middle East Bond Spreads Hit 2022 High as Ceasefire Breaks Down
items.description: Bond investors are demanding the most compensation since October 2022 to fund Middle Eastern governments as fighting flares again between the US and Iran.

items.title: French Power Exports Jump 36% to Record on Nuclear Output Boost
items.description: French power exports surged to a record in the first half of 2026 as a rebound in nuclear output and rising renewable generation left the country with a big surplus of low-cost electricity to sell acr

items.title: Broker Alpaca Raises $435 Million, Explores Prime Broking Entry
items.description: Brokerage and financial infrastructure startup Alpaca has raised $135 million in equity and $300 million in debt, as it looks to expand a business that helps investors trade across financial markets u

items.title: TSMC Says AI Boom Will Go Another Three Years, Defying Skeptics
items.description: The chipmaker is sharply increasing its capital spending and sales projections for the year.

items.title: Goldman’s Bell Sees More Gains for Europe’s AI Buildout Stocks
items.description: Europe’s AI infrastructure shares have further to run as hyperscalers are expected to increase spending again next year, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s Sharon Bell.

items.title: DCC Gets Sweetened Bid From KKR, ECP After Drawn-Out Talks
items.description: KKR & Co. and Energy Capital Partners have sweetened their bid for DCC Plc to more than £5.7 billion ($7.7 billion) and bought extra time to complete what’s become a drawn-out takeover process.

items.title: Nickel Hits Three-Week High on Indonesia Supply Risks, Fed View
items.description: Nickel jumped to a three-week high as expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes faded, while uncertainty over mining policy in top producer Indonesia clouded the supply outlook.

items.title: Gold Declines as Fresh US Strikes on Iran Fuel Inflation Fears
items.description: Gold declined, erasing a moderate gain from the previous session, as escalating tensions in the Middle East fueled concerns that energy prices and inflation will stay high.

items.title: Stocks Fall on AI Trade Jitters as Brent Tops $85: Markets Wrap
items.description: A selloff in chipmakers dragged down stocks on concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations, with the market also falling as higher oil prices lifted

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
items.description: Oil resumed gains as concerns mount about disruptions to flows from the Middle East, with the US continuing to strike Iran.

items.title: Partners Group Slumps as Popular Funds Weigh on Asset Growth
items.description: Partners Group Holding AG shares slumped after the Swiss manager of private equity and credit funds said client redemptions from its popular evergreen vehicles weighed on growth in the first half.
